title: Shiga Prefecture

infobox.value: (滋賀県, *Shiga-ken*; Japanese pronunciation: [[ɕiꜜ.ɡa, -ŋa, ɕi.ɡaꜜ.keɴ, -ŋaꜜ.keɴ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Japanese "Help:IPA/Japanese")[[2]](#cite_note-2)) is a landlocked [prefecture](/wiki/Prefectures_of_Japan "Prefectures of Japan") of Japan in the [Kansai region](/wiki/Kansai_region "Kansai region") of Honshu.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Shiga Prefecture has a population of 1,398,972 as of 1 February 2025 and has a geographic area of 4,017 km2 (1,551 sq mi). Shiga Prefecture borders [Fukui Prefecture](/wiki/Fukui_Prefecture "Fukui Prefecture") to the north, [Gifu Prefecture](/wiki/Gifu_Prefecture "Gifu Prefecture") to the northeast, [Mie Prefecture](/wiki/Mie_Prefecture "Mie Prefecture") to the southeast, and [Kyoto Prefecture](/wiki/Kyoto_Prefecture "Kyoto Prefecture") to the west.

or **Gōshū** before the prefectural system was established.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Omi was a neighbor of [Nara](/wiki/Nara,_Nara "Nara, Nara") and [Kyoto](/wiki/Kyoto "Kyoto"), at the junction of western and eastern Japan. During the period 667 to 672, [Emperor Tenji](/wiki/Emperor_Tenji "Emperor Tenji") founded a palace in Otsu. In 742, [Emperor Shōmu](/wiki/Emperor_Sh%C5%8Dmu "Emperor Shōmu") founded a palace in [Shigaraki](/wiki/Shigaraki,_Shiga "Shigaraki, Shiga"). In the early [Heian period](/wiki/Heian_period "Heian period"), [Saichō](/wiki/Saich%C5%8D "Saichō") was born in the north of Otsu and founded [Enryaku-ji](/wiki/Enryaku-ji "Enryaku-ji"), the center of [Tendai](/wiki/Tendai "Tendai") and a [UNESCO](/wiki/UNESCO "UNESCO") [World Heritage Site](/wiki/World_Heritage_Site "World Heritage Site") [and monument of Ancient Kyoto](/wiki/Historic_Monuments_of_Ancient_Kyoto_(Kyoto,_Uji_and_Otsu_Cities) "Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)") now.

**Shiga Prefecture** (滋賀県, *Shiga-ken*; Japanese pronunciation: [[ɕiꜜ.ɡa, -ŋa, ɕi.ɡaꜜ.keɴ, -ŋaꜜ.keɴ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Japanese "Help:IPA/Japanese")[[2]](#cite_note-2)) is a landlocked [prefecture](/wiki/Prefectures_of_Japan "Prefectures of Japan") of Japan in the [Kansai region](/wiki/Kansai_region "Kansai region") of Honshu.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Shiga Prefecture has a population of 1,398,972 as of 1 February 2025 and has a geographic area of 4,017 km2 (1,551 sq mi). Shiga Prefecture borders [Fukui Prefecture](/wiki/Fukui_Prefecture "Fukui Prefecture") to the north, [Gifu Prefecture](/wiki/Gifu_Prefecture "Gifu Prefecture") to the northeast, [Mie Prefecture](/wiki/Mie_Prefecture "Mie Prefecture") to the southeast, and [Kyoto Prefecture](/wiki/Kyoto_Prefecture "Kyoto Prefecture") to the west.

[Ōtsu](/wiki/%C5%8Ctsu "Ōtsu") is the capital and largest city of Shiga Prefecture, with other major cities including [Kusatsu](/wiki/Kusatsu,_Shiga "Kusatsu, Shiga"), [Nagahama](/wiki/Nagahama,_Shiga "Nagahama, Shiga"), and [Higashiōmi](/wiki/Higashi%C5%8Dmi "Higashiōmi").[[4]](#cite_note-4) Shiga Prefecture encircles [Lake Biwa](/wiki/Lake_Biwa "Lake Biwa"), the largest [freshwater lake](/wiki/Freshwater_lake "Freshwater lake") in Japan, and 37% of the total land area is designated as [Natural Parks](/wiki/List_of_national_parks_of_Japan "List of national parks of Japan"), the highest of any prefecture. Shiga Prefecture's southern half is located adjacent to the former capital city of [Kyoto](/wiki/Kyoto "Kyoto") and forms part of [Greater Kyoto](/wiki/Greater_Kyoto "Greater Kyoto"), the fourth-largest [metropolitan area](/wiki/Metropolitan_area "Metropolitan area") in Japan. Shiga Prefecture is home to [Ōmi beef](/wiki/%C5%8Cmi_beef "Ōmi beef"), the [Eight Views of Ōmi](/wiki/Eight_Views_of_%C5%8Cmi "Eight Views of Ōmi"), and [Hikone Castle](/wiki/Hikone_Castle "Hikone Castle"), one of four [national treasure castles](/wiki/List_of_National_Treasures_of_Japan_(castles) "List of National Treasures of Japan (castles)") in Japan.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shiga_Prefecture&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

See also: [Historic Sites of Shiga Prefecture](/wiki/Historic_Sites_of_Shiga_Prefecture "Historic Sites of Shiga Prefecture")

Shiga was known as **[Ōmi Province](/wiki/%C5%8Cmi_Province "Ōmi Province")** or **Gōshū** before the prefectural system was established.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Omi was a neighbor of [Nara](/wiki/Nara,_Nara "Nara, Nara") and [Kyoto](/wiki/Kyoto "Kyoto"), at the junction of western and eastern Japan. During the period 667 to 672, [Emperor Tenji](/wiki/Emperor_Tenji "Emperor Tenji") founded a palace in Otsu. In 742, [Emperor Shōmu](/wiki/Emperor_Sh%C5%8Dmu "Emperor Shōmu") founded a palace in [Shigaraki](/wiki/Shigaraki,_Shiga "Shigaraki, Shiga"). In the early [Heian period](/wiki/Heian_period "Heian period"), [Saichō](/wiki/Saich%C5%8D "Saichō") was born in the north of Otsu and founded [Enryaku-ji](/wiki/Enryaku-ji "Enryaku-ji"), the center of [Tendai](/wiki/Tendai "Tendai") and a [UNESCO](/wiki/UNESCO "UNESCO") [World Heritage Site](/wiki/World_Heritage_Site "World Heritage Site") [and monument of Ancient Kyoto](/wiki/Historic_Monuments_of_Ancient_Kyoto_(Kyoto,_Uji_and_Otsu_Cities) "Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)") now.

During the [Heian period](/wiki/Heian_period "Heian period"), the [Sasaki clan](/wiki/Sasaki_clan "Sasaki clan") ruled Omi, and afterward, the [Rokkaku clan](/wiki/Rokkaku_clan "Rokkaku clan"), [Kyōgoku clan](/wiki/Ky%C5%8Dgoku_clan "Kyōgoku clan"), and [Azai clans](/wiki/Azai_clan "Azai clan") ruled Omi. While during the [Azuchi-Momoyama period](/wiki/Azuchi%E2%80%93Momoyama_period "Azuchi–Momoyama period"), [Oda Nobunaga](/wiki/Oda_Nobunaga "Oda Nobunaga") subjugated Omi and built [Azuchi Castle](/wiki/Azuchi_Castle "Azuchi Castle") on the eastern shores of [Lake Biwa](/wiki/Lake_Biwa "Lake Biwa") in 1579. [Tōdō Takatora](/wiki/T%C5%8Dd%C5%8D_Takatora "Tōdō Takatora"), [Gamō Ujisato](/wiki/Gam%C5%8D_Ujisato "Gamō Ujisato"), [Oichi](/wiki/Oichi "Oichi"), [Yodo-dono](/wiki/Yodo-dono "Yodo-dono"), [Ohatsu](/wiki/Ohatsu "Ohatsu"), and [Oeyo](/wiki/Oeyo "Oeyo") were Omi notables during this period.

In 1600, [Ishida Mitsunari](/wiki/Ishida_Mitsunari "Ishida Mitsunari"), born in the east of Nagahama and based in [Sawayama Castle](/wiki/Sawayama_Castle "Sawayama Castle"), made war against [Tokugawa Ieyasu](/wiki/Tokugawa_Ieyasu "Tokugawa Ieyasu") in [Sekigahara, Gifu](/wiki/Sekigahara,_Gifu "Sekigahara, Gifu"). After the battle, Ieyasu made [Ii Naomasa](/wiki/Ii_Naomasa "Ii Naomasa") a new lord of Sawayama. Naomasa established the [Hikone Domain](/wiki/Hikone_Domain "Hikone Domain"), later known for [Ii Naosuke](/wiki/Ii_Naosuke "Ii Naosuke"). Ii Naosuke became the Tokugawa shogunate's [Tairō](/wiki/Tair%C5%8D "Tairō") and concluded commercial treaties with the Western powers and thus ended Japan's isolation from the world in the 19th century. Besides the Hikone Domain, many domains ruled Omi such as [Zeze](/wiki/Zeze_Domain "Zeze Domain").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/87/Complete_map_of_Shiga_Prefecture%2C_1880.jpg/250px-Complete_map_of_Shiga_Prefecture%2C_1880.jpg)](/wiki/File:Complete_map_of_Shiga_Prefecture,_1880.jpg)

Map of Shiga Prefecture, 1880

With the [abolition of the han system](/wiki/Abolition_of_the_han_system "Abolition of the han system"), eight prefectures were formed in Omi. They were unified into Shiga Prefecture in September 1872. "Shiga Prefecture" was named after "[Shiga District](/wiki/Shiga_District,_Shiga "Shiga District, Shiga")" because Otsu belonged to the district until 1898. From August 1876 to February 1881, southern Fukui Prefecture had been incorporated into Shiga Prefecture.

In 2015, Shiga Governor [Taizō Mikazuki](/wiki/Taiz%C5%8D_Mikazuki "Taizō Mikazuki") conducted a survey asking citizens whether they felt it necessary to change the name of the prefecture, partly to raise its profile as a destination for domestic tourism.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shiga_Prefecture&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/df/Lake_biwa.jpg/250px-Lake_biwa.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lake_biwa.jpg)

Lake Biwa in Shiga Prefecture viewed from space

Shiga borders [Fukui Prefecture](/wiki/Fukui_Prefecture "Fukui Prefecture") in the north, [Gifu Prefecture](/wiki/Gifu_Prefecture "Gifu Prefecture") in the east, [Mie Prefecture](/wiki/Mie_Prefecture "Mie Prefecture") in the southeast, and [Kyoto Prefecture](/wiki/Kyoto_Prefecture "Kyoto Prefecture") in the west.

Lake Biwa, Japan's largest, is located at the center of this prefecture. It occupies one-sixth of its area. The [Seta River](/wiki/Seta_River "Seta River") flows from Lake Biwa to [Osaka Bay](/wiki/Osaka_Bay "Osaka Bay") through [Kyoto](/wiki/Kyoto "Kyoto"). This is the only natural river that flows out from the lake. Most other natural rivers flow into the lake. There were many lagoons around Lake Biwa, but most of them were reclaimed in 1940s. One of the preserved lagoons is the wetland (水郷, *suigō*) in Omihachiman, and it was selected as the first [Important Cultural Landscapes](/wiki/Cultural_Landscapes_of_Japan "Cultural Landscapes of Japan") in 2006.

The lake divides the prefecture into four different areas: *Kohoku* (湖北; north of lake) centered Nagahama, *Kosei* (湖西; west of lake) centered [Imazu](/wiki/Imazu,_Shiga "Imazu, Shiga"), *Kotō* (湖東; east of lake) centered Hikone and *Konan* (湖南; south of lake) centered Otsu.

Plains stretch to the eastern shore of Lake Biwa. The prefecture is enclosed by mountain ranges with the [Hira Mountains](/wiki/Hira_Mountains "Hira Mountains") and [Mount Hiei](/wiki/Mount_Hiei "Mount Hiei") in the west, the [Ibuki Mountains](/wiki/Ibuki_Mountains "Ibuki Mountains") in the northeast, and the [Suzuka Mountains](/wiki/Suzuka_Mountains "Suzuka Mountains") in the southeast. [Mount Ibuki](/wiki/Mount_Ibuki "Mount Ibuki") is the highest mountain in Shiga. In [Yogo](/wiki/Yogo,_Shiga "Yogo, Shiga"), a small lake known for the legend of the heavenly robe of an angel (天女の羽衣, *[tennyo](/wiki/Tennin "Tennin") no hagoromo*), which is similar to a western [Swan maiden](/wiki/Swan_maiden "Swan maiden").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Shiga's climate sharply varies between north and south. Southern Shiga is usually warm, but northern Shiga is typically cold with high snowfall and hosts many skiing grounds. In Nakanokawachi, the northernmost village of Shiga, snow reached a depth of 5.6 metres (18 ft) in 1936.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

As of 1 April 2014, 37% of the total land area of the prefecture was designated as [Natural Parks](/wiki/List_of_national_parks_of_Japan#History "List of national parks of Japan") (the highest total of any prefecture), namely the [Biwako](/wiki/Biwako_Quasi-National_Park "Biwako Quasi-National Park") and [Suzuka](/wiki/Suzuka_Quasi-National_Park "Suzuka Quasi-National Park") Quasi-National Parks; and [Kotō](/wiki/Kot%C5%8D_Prefectural_Natural_Park "Kotō Prefectural Natural Park"), [Kutsuki-Katsuragawa](/wiki/Kutsuki-Katsuragawa_Prefectural_Natural_Park "Kutsuki-Katsuragawa Prefectural Natural Park"), and [Mikami-Tanakami-Shigaraki](/wiki/Mikami-Tanakami-Shigaraki_Prefectural_Natural_Park "Mikami-Tanakami-Shigaraki Prefectural Natural Park") Prefectural Natural Parks.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shiga_Prefecture&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Politics")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b2/Shiga_Prefectural_Government_Office.jpg/250px-Shiga_Prefectural_Government_Office.jpg)](/wiki/File:Shiga_Prefectural_Government_Office.jpg)

Ōtsu City's prefectural government building

[Taizō Mikazuki](/wiki/Taiz%C5%8D_Mikazuki "Taizō Mikazuki"), a former member of the House of Representatives from Shiga, was narrowly [elected governor in July 2014](/wiki/2014_Shiga_gubernatorial_election "2014 Shiga gubernatorial election") with center-left support against ex-METI-bureaucrat [Takashi Koyari](/wiki/Takashi_Koyari "Takashi Koyari") (supported by the center-right national-level ruling parties) to succeed governor [Yukiko Kada](/wiki/Yukiko_Kada "Yukiko Kada"). In June 2018, he was [overwhelmingly reelected to a second term](/wiki/2018_Shiga_gubernatorial_election "2018 Shiga gubernatorial election") against one challenger, a communist.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)

The [prefectural assembly](/w/index.php?title=Shiga_Prefectural_Assembly&action=edit&redlink=1 "Shiga Prefectural Assembly (page does not exist)") has 44 members from 16 electoral districts, and is elected in unified local elections. As of July 2019, the assembly was composed by caucus as follows: [LDP](/wiki/Liberal_Democratic_Party_of_Japan "Liberal Democratic Party of Japan") 20 members, Team Shiga ([CDP](/wiki/Constitutional_Democratic_Party_of_Japan "Constitutional Democratic Party of Japan"), [DPP](/wiki/Democratic_Party_for_the_People "Democratic Party for the People"), former Kada supporters etc.) 14, [JCP](/wiki/Japanese_Communist_Party "Japanese Communist Party") 4, Sazanami Club (of independents) 3, [Kōmeitō](/wiki/K%C5%8Dmeit%C5%8D "Kōmeitō") 2, "independent"/non-attached 1.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

In the [National Diet](/wiki/National_Diet "National Diet"), Shiga is represented by four directly elected members of the [House of Representatives](/wiki/House_of_Representatives_(Japan) "House of Representatives (Japan)") and two (one per ordinary election) of the [House of Councillors](/wiki/House_of_Councillors "House of Councillors"). For the proportional representation segment of the lower house, the prefecture forms part of the [Kinki block](/wiki/Kinki_proportional_representation_block "Kinki proportional representation block"). After the national elections of [2016](/wiki/2016_Japanese_House_of_Councillors_election "2016 Japanese House of Councillors election"), [2017](/w/index.php?title=2017_Japanese_House_of_Representatives_election&action=edit&redlink=1 "2017 Japanese House of Representatives election (page does not exist)") and [2019](/wiki/2019_Japanese_House_of_Councillors_election "2019 Japanese House of Councillors election"), the directly elected delegation to the Diet from Shiga consists of (as of August 1, 2019):

* in the House of Representatives
  + for the [1st district](/wiki/Shiga_1st_district "Shiga 1st district") in the west: [Toshitaka Ōoka](/wiki/Toshitaka_%C5%8Coka "Toshitaka Ōoka"), LDP, 3rd term,
  + for the [2nd district](/wiki/Shiga_2nd_district "Shiga 2nd district") in the northeast: [Ken'ichirō Ueno](/wiki/Ken%27ichir%C5%8D_Ueno "Ken'ichirō Ueno"), LDP, 4th term,
  + for the [3rd district](/wiki/Shiga_3rd_district "Shiga 3rd district") on the southern shores of Lake Biwa: [Nobuhide Takemura](/wiki/Nobuhide_Takemura "Nobuhide Takemura"), LDP, 3rd term,
  + for the [4th district](/w/index.php?title=Shiga_4th_district&action=edit&redlink=1 "Shiga 4th district (page does not exist)") in the southeast: [Hiroo Kotera](/wiki/Hiroo_Kotera "Hiroo Kotera"), LDP, 1st term,
* in the House of Councillors ([Shiga At-large district](/w/index.php?title=Shiga_At-large_district&action=edit&redlink=1 "Shiga At-large district (page does not exist)"))
  + in the class of 2016 (term ends 2022): Takashi Koyari, LDP, 1st term,
  + in the class of 2019 (term ends 2025): Yukiko Kada, independent sitting with the [Hekisuikai](/wiki/Hekisuikai "Hekisuikai") caucus, 1st term.

In 2020, Shiga Prefecture had the highest [life expectancy fixed in Japan](/wiki/List_of_Japanese_prefectures_by_life_expectancy "List of Japanese prefectures by life expectancy"): 85.71 years[[15]](#cite_note-15)

The population is concentrated along the southern shore of Lake Biwa in Otsu city (adjacent to Kyoto) and along the lake's eastern shore in cities such as Kusatsu and Moriyama, which are [within commuting distance to Kyoto](/wiki/Greater_Kyoto "Greater Kyoto"). The lake's western and northern shores are more rural and resort-oriented with white sand beaches. In recent years, many Brazilians settled in Shiga to work in nearby factories. 25,040 foreigners live in Shiga and 30% of foreigners were Brazilians as of December 2016.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shiga_Prefecture&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Culture")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5b/Sagawa_art_museum01s3200.jpg/250px-Sagawa_art_museum01s3200.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sagawa_art_museum01s3200.jpg)

Sagawa Art Museum

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9e/Lake_Biwa_Aquarium.jpg/250px-Lake_Biwa_Aquarium.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lake_Biwa_Aquarium.jpg)

Aquarium of Lake Biwa Museum

[Biwa Town](/wiki/Biwa,_Shiga "Biwa, Shiga") (a part of Nagahama) is a home of The [Tonda Traditional Bunraku Puppet Troupe](/wiki/Tonda_Traditional_Bunraku_Puppet_Troupe "Tonda Traditional Bunraku Puppet Troupe").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Museums include the [Sagawa Art Museum](/wiki/Sagawa_Art_Museum "Sagawa Art Museum") in Moriyama, the [Lake Biwa Museum](/wiki/Lake_Biwa_Museum "Lake Biwa Museum") in Kusatsu and the [Miho Museum](/wiki/Miho_Museum "Miho Museum") in Kōka. In Kōka, a ninja house is preserved as a visitor center.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

### Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shiga_Prefecture&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Education")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b2/The_University_of_Shiga_Prefecture.JPG/250px-The_University_of_Shiga_Prefecture.JPG)](/wiki/File:The_University_of_Shiga_Prefecture.JPG)

University of Shiga Prefecture

Ten universities, two junior colleges, and a learning center of [The Open University of Japan](/wiki/The_Open_University_of_Japan "The Open University of Japan") operate in Shiga.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

* [Biwako-Gakuin University](/wiki/Newton_College_(Japan) "Newton College (Japan)") (Higashiomi)
* [Biwako Seikei Sport College](/wiki/Biwako_Seikei_Sport_College "Biwako Seikei Sport College") (Otsu)
* [Nagahama Institute of Bio-Science and Technology](/wiki/Nagahama_Institute_of_Bio-Science_and_Technology "Nagahama Institute of Bio-Science and Technology") (Nagahama)
* [Ritsumeikan University](/wiki/Ritsumeikan_University "Ritsumeikan University") (Kyoto and Kusatsu)
* [Ryukoku University](/wiki/Ryukoku_University "Ryukoku University") (Kyoto and Otsu)
* [Seian University of Art and Design](/wiki/Seian_University_of_Art_and_Design "Seian University of Art and Design") (Otsu)
* [Seisen University](/wiki/Seisen_University_(Shiga) "Seisen University (Shiga)") (Hikone)
* [Shiga Bunkyo Junior College](/wiki/Shiga_Bunkyo_Junior_College "Shiga Bunkyo Junior College") (Nagahama)
* [Shiga Junior College](/wiki/Shiga_Junior_College "Shiga Junior College") (Otsu)
* [Shiga University](/wiki/Shiga_University "Shiga University") (Hikone and [Ōtsu](/wiki/%C5%8Ctsu "Ōtsu"))
* [Shiga University of Medical Science](/wiki/Shiga_University_of_Medical_Science "Shiga University of Medical Science") (Otsu)
* [University of Shiga Prefecture](/wiki/University_of_Shiga_Prefecture "University of Shiga Prefecture") (Hikone)

An example of the educational content that is unique to Shiga Prefecture is [Biwako Floating School](/wiki/Biwako_Floating_School "Biwako Floating School"), also known as *Uminoko*.[[19]](#cite_note-Yoshikawa_(2008)-19) Biwako Floating School is the project for running an educational cruise program in which fifth grade pupils of elementary schools living in Shiga board a ship *Uminoko* on Lake Biwa and learn about the environment and ecosystem of the lake.[[19]](#cite_note-Yoshikawa_(2008)-19)

### Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shiga_Prefecture&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Sports")]

The following sports teams are based in Shiga.

* Basketball: Shiga LakeStars
* Football (soccer): [Lagend Shiga](/wiki/Lagend_Shiga_FC "Lagend Shiga FC") (Moriyama), MIO Biwako Kusastsu (later [Reilac Shiga](/wiki/Reilac_Shiga_FC "Reilac Shiga FC")) (Kusatsu), [Sagawa Shiga F.C.](/wiki/Sagawa_Shiga_F.C. "Sagawa Shiga F.C.") (Moriyama).
* Tennis: SHRIGGA AKA UVEAL
* Volleyball: [Toray Arrows (women's volleyball team)](/wiki/Toray_Arrows_(women%27s_volleyball_team) "Toray Arrows (women's volleyball team)") (Otsu)

### Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shiga_Prefecture&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Transport")]

There are no airports within the prefecture itself. However, airports such as [Chubu Centrair International Airport](/wiki/Chubu_Centrair_International_Airport "Chubu Centrair International Airport"), [Itami Airport](/wiki/Itami_Airport "Itami Airport"), and [Kansai International Airport](/wiki/Kansai_International_Airport "Kansai International Airport") are also used by air travellers from the prefecture.

## Tourism

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shiga_Prefecture&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Tourism")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f8/Mangetsuji_Otsu_Shiga02n4500.jpg/250px-Mangetsuji_Otsu_Shiga02n4500.jpg)](/wiki/File:Mangetsuji_Otsu_Shiga02n4500.jpg)

Ukimidō hall at [Mangetsu-ji](/wiki/Mangetsu-ji "Mangetsu-ji") temple near Katata, Ōtsu

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9b/Michigan_-_Lake_Biwa%2C_Japan_-_DSC07330.JPG/250px-Michigan_-_Lake_Biwa%2C_Japan_-_DSC07330.JPG)](/wiki/File:Michigan_-_Lake_Biwa,_Japan_-_DSC07330.JPG)

From Otsu port, the Michigan paddlewheel boat offers cruises on Lake Biwa

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2c/Hikone-jo-r.jpg/250px-Hikone-jo-r.jpg)](/wiki/File:Hikone-jo-r.jpg)

[Hikone Castle](/wiki/Hikone_Castle "Hikone Castle")

In 2000 sixty-five thousand tourists visited Shiga.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

Festivals include the *hikiyama matsuri* ([曳山祭](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E9%95%B7%E6%B5%9C%E6%9B%B3%E5%B1%B1%E7%A5%AD "jp:長浜曳山祭"); floats parade) festival, held in ten areas including [Nagahama](/wiki/Nagahama,_Shiga "Nagahama, Shiga") each April, one of the three major hikiyama festivals in Japan, which was designated an Important Intangible Cultural Property in 1979. During the festival ornate floats are mounted with miniature stages on which boys (playing both male and female roles) act in [kabuki](/wiki/Kabuki "Kabuki") plays.[[21]](#cite_note-21)
